title: AS-IS SaaS Agreement with No Warranties and Vendor-Favorable Termination

issues.rationale: The AS-IS/no-warranty provision disclaims all implied warranties (merchantability, fitness, title, non-infringement). In a paid SaaS agreement, this creates significant risk for the customer who is paying for a service with no guarantee of quality or functionality.
No SLA means no guaranteed uptime, availability, or performance. Customer's only remedy for service interruption is termination, which is inadequate for a business-critical service.

must_contain_quoted_text_pattern: terminate this Agreement at any time
issues.rationale: Provider can terminate for convenience with 30 days notice, while Customer can only terminate for material breach. This creates a fundamental power imbalance where the customer's business depends on a service the provider can cancel at will.

ground_truth.rationale: Three distinct issues: (1) AS-IS disclaimer eliminates all warranties for a paid service, (2) no SLA leaves customer without remedy for service failures, (3) asymmetric termination allows vendor to cancel at will while locking customer in. The no-indemnification clause is also notable but may not be separately flagged if the AS-IS clause already covers it.
